heading: Step 3: Delete the service account.
body:
- Click the Trash icon under **Actions**
  to the right of the service account you want to delete.

- Click **Delete**.

  Deleting a service account from an organization also removes
  it from from any projects to which the service account was
  granted access.

To revoke programmatic access to an organization through the Atlas Administration API,
you can delete any existing service accounts for that organization.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to:

- [Delete a service account secret](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deleteorgserviceaccountsecret).

- [Delete a service account](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deleteorgserviceaccount).

To revoke programmatic access to an organization through the Atlas Administration API,
you can delete any existing API (Application Programming Interface) keys for that organization.

Removing an API (Application Programming Interface) Key from an Organization also removes that key from
any projects to which the key was granted access.

To revoke programmatic access to an organization through the Atlas Administration API,
you can delete any existing API (Application Programming Interface) keys for that organization.

To delete an API key from an organization using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys delete <ID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-delete).

To delete an access list entry for an API (Application Programming Interface) key in an
organization, see [Update Programmatic Access to an Organization](change-org-api-key-roles).
